entries.name: thread/fork
entries.description: There are two ways to fork a thread: 1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and fork it into a new thread. 2. By path: load the thread from disk by path and fork it into a new thread. If using a non-empty path, the thread_id param will be ignored. Empty string path values are treated as absent. Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

entries.name: thread/resume
entries.description: There are three ways to resume a thread: 1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and resume it. 2. By history: instantiate the thread from memory and resume it. 3. By path: load the thread from disk by path and resume it. For non-running threads, the precedence is: history > non-empty path > thread_id. If using history or a non-empty path for a non-running thread, the thread_id param will be ignored. If thread_id identifies a running thread, app-server rejoins that thread and treats a non-empty path as a consistency check against the active rollout path. Empty string path values are treated as absent. Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

result.note: The generated request union associates method with params only; it does not authoritatively associate a result schema.

entries.name: activeTurnNotSteerable
entries.description: This translation layer make sure that we expose codex error code in camel case. When an upstream HTTP status is available (for example, from the Responses API or a provider), it is forwarded in `httpStatusCode` on the relevant `codexErrorInfo` variant. Returned when `turn/start` or `turn/steer` is submitted while the current active turn cannot accept same-turn steering, for example `/review` or manual `/compact`.

entries.name: accept
entries.description: User approved the command.

entries.name: acceptForSession
entries.description: User approved the command and future prompts in the same session-scoped approval cache should run without prompting.

entries.name: acceptWithExecpolicyAmendment
entries.description: User approved the command, and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future matching commands can run without prompting.

entries.name: applyNetworkPolicyAmendment
entries.description: User chose a persistent network policy rule (allow/deny) for this host.

entries.name: cancel
entries.description: User denied the command. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

entries.name: decline
entries.description: User denied the command. The agent will continue the turn.

entries.name: enterpriseManaged
entries.description: Enterprise-managed config layer delivered by the cloud config bundle.

entries.name: legacyManagedConfigTomlFromFile
entries.description: `managed_config.toml` was designed to be a config that was loaded as the last layer on top of everything else. This scheme did not quite work out as intended, but we keep this variant as a "best effort" while we phase out `managed_config.toml` in favor of `requirements.toml`.

entries.description: Managed preferences layer delivered by MDM (macOS only).

entries.name: project
entries.description: Path to a .codex/ folder within a project. There could be multiple of these between `cwd` and the project/repo root.

entries.name: sessionFlags
entries.description: Session-layer overrides supplied via `-c`/`--config`.

entries.name: system
entries.description: Managed config layer from a file (usually `managed_config.toml`).

entries.name: user
entries.description: User config layer from $CODEX_HOME/config.toml. This layer is special in that it is expected to be: - writable by the user - generally outside the workspace directory

entries.name: encrypted_content
entries.description: Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call. This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

entries.name: input_image
entries.description: Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call. This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

entries.name: input_text
entries.description: Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call. This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

entries.name: chatgptAuthTokens
entries.description: [UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE. The access token must contain the same scopes that Codex-managed ChatGPT auth tokens have.

entries.name: custom
entries.description: Controls the effective multi-agent delegation instructions for a turn. `custom` means the configured mode hint defines the policy instead of a built-in policy.

entries.name: explicitRequestOnly
entries.description: Controls the effective multi-agent delegation instructions for a turn. `custom` means the configured mode hint defines the policy instead of a built-in policy.

entries.name: proactive
entries.description: Controls the effective multi-agent delegation instructions for a turn. `custom` means the configured mode hint defines the policy instead of a built-in policy.

entries.name: remote
entries.description: The plugin is available in the remote catalog. Download metadata is kept server-side and is not exposed through the app-server API.

entries.name: abort
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User has denied this command and the agent should not do anything until the user's next command.

entries.name: approved
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User has approved this command and the agent should execute it.

entries.name: approved_execpolicy_amendment
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User has approved this command and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future matching commands are permitted.

entries.name: approved_for_session
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User has approved this request and wants future prompts in the same session-scoped approval cache to be automatically approved for the remainder of the session.

entries.name: denied
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User has denied this command and the agent should not execute it, but it should continue the session and try something else.

entries.name: network_policy_amendment
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. User chose to persist a network policy rule (allow/deny) for future requests to the same host.

entries.name: timed_out
entries.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest. Automatic approval review timed out before reaching a decision.

entries.name: baseBranch
entries.description: Review changes between the current branch and the given base branch.

entries.name: commit
entries.description: Review the changes introduced by a specific commit.

entries.name: custom
entries.description: Arbitrary instructions, equivalent to the old free-form prompt.

entries.name: uncommittedChanges
entries.description: Review the working tree: staged, unstaged, and untracked files.

entries.name: webrtc
entries.description: EXPERIMENTAL - transport used by thread realtime.

entries.name: websocket
entries.description: EXPERIMENTAL - transport used by thread realtime.
